name: new_nonempty_domain
code:
proof fn phi_1_new_nonempty_domain<K: KeyTrait + VerusClone>(s: StrictlyOrderedMap<K>)
    requires
        s.valid(),
        s@ == Map::<K,ID>::empty(),
    ensures
        s@.dom().len() > 0,
{
}
reason: A freshly constructed map must have an empty domain; provability would mean the spec allows non-empty initial state

name: new_contains_arbitrary_key
code:
proof fn phi_2_new_contains_arbitrary_key<K: KeyTrait + VerusClone>(s: StrictlyOrderedMap<K>, k: K)
    requires
        s.valid(),
        s@ == Map::<K,ID>::empty(),
    ensures
        s@.contains_key(k),
{
}
reason: An empty map must not contain any key; provability would indicate the empty-map postcondition is inconsistent with containment semantics

name: valid_contradicts_empty
code:
proof fn phi_3_valid_contradicts_empty<K: KeyTrait + VerusClone>(s: StrictlyOrderedMap<K>)
    requires
        s.valid(),
        s@ == Map::<K,ID>::empty(),
    ensures
        !s.valid(),
{
}
reason: valid() and empty view are co-guaranteed; if both requires lead to !valid(), the ensures of new are mutually contradictory

name: new_domain_off_by_one
code:
proof fn phi_4_new_domain_off_by_one<K: KeyTrait + VerusClone>(s: StrictlyOrderedMap<K>)
    requires
        s.valid(),
        s@ == Map::<K,ID>::empty(),
    ensures
        s@.dom().len() == 1,
{
}
reason: An empty map domain must have length 0 not 1; provability would reveal an off-by-one error in the validity or emptiness specification

name: empty_map_not_finite_domain
code:
proof fn phi_5_empty_map_not_finite_domain<K: KeyTrait + VerusClone>(s: StrictlyOrderedMap<K>)
    requires
        s.valid(),
        s@ == Map::<K,ID>::empty(),
    ensures
        !s@.dom().finite(),
{
}
reason: The empty map's domain is trivially finite; provability would mean map_valid's finiteness invariant is contradicted by the view spec
